# bmad-module-skill-forge — SKF Pipeline Ladder — Epic Breakdown

## Overview

This document provides the complete epic and story breakdown for SKF Pipeline Ladder (v1.9 deepwiki + v2.0 campaign), decomposing the requirements from the PRD and Architecture into implementable stories.

## Requirements Inventory

### Functional Requirements

FR-1: System registers `deepwiki` as a pipeline alias expanding to `AN[auto] BS[auto] CS TS[min:90] EX`
FR-2: AN operates in auto-scope mode — analyzes source, detects skill shape, determines scope without manual configuration
FR-3: AN classifies repos into one of 4 skill shapes (library-API, reference-app/CLI, language-reference, stack/compose) with `unknown` fallback
FR-4: BS generates a brief from AN output + doc detection results, targeting 70%+ success rate (stretch 80%)
FR-5: User validates auto-brief via 10-line summary with progressive fallback to interactive BS
FR-7: Doc detection chain: gh homepageUrl → README link scanning → Pages API → docs/ folder
FR-8: Doc detection implemented as standalone shared module (`skf-detect-docs.py`) callable by any pipeline
FR-9: CS records all detected doc pages + README with content hashes in `metadata.json`
FR-10: AS compares current content hashes against stored hashes on every audit, flags stale skills
FR-11: Tiered quality gate — hard gate (zero critical/high) + soft gate (coverage percentage)
FR-12: Per-pipeline quality thresholds (deepwiki=90%, forge=80%), configurable via `TS[min:N]`
FR-13: Evidence report generated when soft gate falls back from 90% to 80%
FR-14: Post-compile auto-shard splits SKILL.md body exceeding 400 lines to `references/`
FR-15: Auto-decomposition when AN detects >500 exports or >3 packages [PENDING VALIDATION]
FR-16: `_known-workarounds.yaml` schema with fingerprint-to-fix pairs (versioned, validated)
FR-17: Pre-apply step runs before every pipeline iteration, matching fingerprints and applying fixes
FR-18: Registry seeded with 25 GMC campaign fingerprints
FR-19: Post-compile hook in CS greps feeder artifacts for corrected items, annotates with correction blocks
FR-20: `deepwiki <doc-url>` accepts documentation URL without repo for docs-only wiki skills
FR-21: deepwiki detects existing skills before generating, offers merge/alongside/skip
FR-22: `onboard` alias emits deprecation notice directing users to `deepwiki` (v1.9)
FR-23: `onboard` alias removed entirely, returns clear error with pointer (v2.0)
FR-24: `skf-campaign` registered as SKF's 15th workflow, invoked via `campaign`
FR-25: 10-stage pipeline: Setup → Strategy → Pins → Provenance → Skill Loop → Batch → Capstone → Verify → Refine → Export+Maintenance
FR-26: `_campaign-state.yaml` as single source of truth, human-readable YAML, survives context death
FR-27: `_campaign-state.yaml.bak` written before every state transition (one-deep backup)
FR-28: Topological sort from declared `depends_on` edges, circular dependency detection
FR-29: Campaign refuses to start skill whose dependencies haven't passed
FR-30: `campaign resume --from=<skill>` reads state, skips completed, continues
FR-31: `_campaign-directive.md` as file-based standing directive, read at each stage transition
FR-32: `campaign-validate-pins.py` validates version pins against gh releases and registries
FR-33: Kickoff emitter generates fresh-context handoff messages from campaign state
FR-34: Post-campaign report: skills completed, quality scores, findings, workarounds, doc-rot, time
FR-35: Machine-generated `campaign-brief.yaml` at Stage 0 for fresh-context resume
FR-36: `deepwiki --pin <version>` targets specific version using shared pin validation (FR-32)

### NonFunctional Requirements

NFR-1: Backward compatibility — no existing workflow breaks at either release
NFR-2: File-based state persistence — campaign state 100% recoverable from disk, no memory dependency
NFR-3: Shared module reusability — detect-docs, shape-detect, pre-apply, auto-shard are standalone in `src/shared/scripts/`
NFR-4: Graceful degradation — every auto feature has defined fallback (auto-brief→interactive, 90%→80%, etc.)
NFR-5: Human readability — YAML state, markdown reports, hand-recovery possible without tooling
NFR-6: Provenance, not generation — no feature may dilute source-grounded extraction
NFR-7: `-wiki` suffix enforcement — pipeline-enforced semantic metadata (coverage, quality, update semantics)
NFR-8: Pipeline alias as contract — alias definitions are public surface, changes are breaking

### Additional Requirements

From Architecture document:
- Campaign headless envelope: `SKF_CAMPAIGN_RESULT_JSON` at terminal step
- Mode flag mechanics: `[auto]` modifier passed via pipeline data context
- Shared module interfaces: `skf-detect-docs.py`, `skf-shape-detect.py`, `skf-preapply.py` — all via `uv run`, JSON stdout, exit codes 0/1/2
- Campaign state schema: JSON Schema validation at `assets/campaign-state-schema.json`
- Step file patterns: stateless interpreters, read-backup-modify-write for campaign
- Per-pipeline threshold lookup table in TS first step
- Kickoff emitter template contract at `templates/kickoff-template.md`
- Pre-apply registry merge: shared seed + project-local, ephemeral merge at invocation
- Implementation sequence: shared modules → AN/BS auto → CS/AS enhancements → TS gates → deepwiki alias → campaign schema → campaign steps
- SS active_version manifest-state bugfix (v1.9 scope per brainstorming BH-8)

### UX Design Requirements

Not applicable — CLI/agent tool with no UI surface.

### FR Coverage Map

| FR | Epic | Description |
|----|------|-------------|
| FR-3 | Epic 1 | Skill-shape detection (shared module) |
| FR-7 | Epic 1 | Doc detection chain |
| FR-8 | Epic 1 | Doc detection as shared module |
| FR-9 | Epic 1 | Doc tracking at compile time |
| FR-10 | Epic 1 | Doc drift detection |
| FR-1 | Epic 2 | deepwiki pipeline alias registration |
| FR-2 | Epic 2 | Auto-scope mode |
| FR-4 | Epic 2 | Auto-brief generation |
| FR-5 | Epic 2 | Auto-brief validation + progressive fallback |
| FR-15 | Epic 2 | Auto-decomposition for massive repos |
| FR-20 | Epic 2 | Docs-only path |
| FR-21 | Epic 2 | Coexistence detection |
| FR-36 | Epic 2 | Version pinning |
| FR-22 | Epic 2 | onboard deprecation (v1.9) |
| FR-11 | Epic 3 | Tiered quality gate |
| FR-12 | Epic 3 | Per-pipeline quality thresholds |
| FR-13 | Epic 3 | Evidence report on fallback |
| FR-14 | Epic 3 | Auto-shard at 400-line ceiling |
| FR-16 | Epic 3 | Workaround registry schema |
| FR-17 | Epic 3 | Auto-apply before pipeline runs |
| FR-18 | Epic 3 | GMC seed data (25 fingerprints) |
| FR-19 | Epic 3 | Doc-rot correction hooks |
| FR-24 | Epic 4 | Campaign workflow registration |
| FR-25 | Epic 4 | 10-stage pipeline |
| FR-26 | Epic 4 | File-based campaign state |
| FR-27 | Epic 4 | State file backup |
| FR-28 | Epic 4 | Dependency tracking |
| FR-29 | Epic 4 | Dependency enforcement |
| FR-30 | Epic 4 | Resume from any skill |
| FR-31 | Epic 4 | Standing directive |
| FR-32 | Epic 4 | Pin validation |
| FR-33 | Epic 4 | Kickoff emitter |
| FR-34 | Epic 4 | Campaign report |
| FR-35 | Epic 4 | Campaign-brief.yaml |
| FR-23 | Epic 4 | onboard removal (v2.0) |

## Epic List

### Epic 1: Source Intelligence Foundation
SKF understands sources better — detects documentation URLs, classifies repo shapes, tracks upstream doc changes, flags stale skills. Creates the shared modules that all downstream epics depend on.
**FRs covered:** FR-3, FR-7, FR-8, FR-9, FR-10 + SS active_version bugfix

### Epic 2: deepwiki — Zero-Ceremony Skill Creation
Run `deepwiki <repo-url>` and get a 90%-coverage wiki skill in 3-5 minutes. Also works for doc URLs, specific versions, and detects existing skills before overwriting. Includes v1.9 documentation and onboard deprecation.
**FRs covered:** FR-1, FR-2, FR-4, FR-5, FR-15, FR-20, FR-21, FR-36, FR-22

### Epic 3: Quality, Scale & Workaround Automation
Higher quality skills with transparent quality gates, automatic handling of large outputs, and no more re-discovering workarounds across pipeline runs. Core improvements that benefit all pipelines.
**FRs covered:** FR-11, FR-12, FR-13, FR-14, FR-16, FR-17, FR-18, FR-19

### Epic 4: Campaign Orchestration (v2.0)
Orchestrate 15+ coordinated skills across multiple sessions with dependency tracking, file-based state, and resume from any skill. Includes v2.0 documentation and onboard removal.
**FRs covered:** FR-24, FR-25, FR-26, FR-27, FR-28, FR-29, FR-30, FR-31, FR-32, FR-33, FR-34, FR-35, FR-23
# SKF Pipeline Ladder — Epic 1 & 2 Story Breakdown

---

## Epic 1: Source Intelligence Foundation

**Goal:** SKF understands sources better — detects documentation URLs, classifies repo shapes, tracks upstream doc changes, flags stale skills.

**FRs:** FR-3, FR-7, FR-8, FR-9, FR-10 + SS active_version bugfix

---

### Story 1.1: Skill-shape detection shared module

As a pipeline author,
I want a standalone shape-detection script that classifies repos into known skill shapes,
So that AN auto-scope, BS auto-brief, and TS threshold selection can all consume the same classification without duplicating heuristics.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** a repo URL and one or more manifest paths (package.json, pyproject.toml, Cargo.toml)
**When** `uv run python src/shared/scripts/skf-shape-detect.py --repo-url <url> --manifests <paths>` is invoked
**Then** stdout contains a JSON object `{shape, signals[], confidence, export_count, package_count}`
**And** `shape` is one of `library-API`, `reference-app`, `language-reference`, `stack-compose`
**And** exit code is `0`

**Given** a repo whose manifests and structure do not match any known shape
**When** shape detection is invoked
**Then** `shape` is `unknown`
**And** exit code is `1`

**Given** an invalid repo URL or missing manifest paths
**When** shape detection is invoked
**Then** stderr contains `{"error": "...", "code": "ERROR_TYPE"}`
**And** exit code is `2`

**Given** the test suite at `test/test-skf-shape-detect.py`
**When** `uv run pytest test/test-skf-shape-detect.py` is executed
**Then** all tests pass
**And** the test file is registered in `package.json` test:python script

**Technical Notes:**
- File: `src/shared/scripts/skf-shape-detect.py`
- Test: `test/test-skf-shape-detect.py`
- Follow existing shared script pattern (skf-preflight.py, skf-detect-language.py): args via argparse, JSON stdout, stderr errors, exit codes 0/1/2
- Signals array contains the evidence that drove the classification (e.g., `["has_package_json", "exports_count_gt_50", "no_bin_field"]`)
- Confidence is a float 0.0-1.0

---

### Story 1.2: Doc detection chain shared module

As a pipeline author,
I want a standalone doc-detection script that discovers documentation URLs through multiple methods,
So that BS, CS, AS, and campaign workflows can all locate a repo's docs without duplicating detection logic.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** a repo URL with a `homepageUrl` set on GitHub
**When** `uv run python src/shared/scripts/skf-detect-docs.py --repo-url <url>` is invoked
**Then** stdout contains a JSON array including an entry with `detected_via: "homepageUrl"`
**And** exit code is `0`

**Given** a repo whose README contains documentation links
**When** doc detection is invoked
**Then** the output array includes entries with `detected_via: "readme_link"`
**And** each entry has `url`, `content_hash` (SHA-256 hex lowercase), and `content_type`

**Given** a repo with a GitHub Pages site
**When** doc detection is invoked without `--skip-pages-api`
**Then** the output array includes an entry with `detected_via: "pages_api"`

**Given** a repo with a `docs/` folder containing markdown files
**When** doc detection is invoked
**Then** the output array includes entries with `detected_via: "docs_folder"`

**Given** a repo whose README links to a CHANGELOG.md or migration guide
**When** doc detection is invoked
**Then** changelogs and migration guides are excluded from the output array

**Given** all four detection methods find zero documentation sources
**When** doc detection is invoked
**Then** stdout contains an empty JSON array `[]`
**And** exit code is `1`

**Given** a `--local-path` argument alongside `--repo-url`
**When** doc detection is invoked
**Then** docs/ folder detection uses the local path instead of cloning

**Given** the test suite at `test/test-skf-detect-docs.py`
**When** `uv run pytest test/test-skf-detect-docs.py` is executed
**Then** all tests pass
**And** the test file is registered in `package.json` test:python script

**Technical Notes:**
- File: `src/shared/scripts/skf-detect-docs.py`
- Test: `test/test-skf-detect-docs.py`
- All four detection methods are attempted and results aggregated (not short-circuited)
- Output schema: `[{url: string, detected_via: string, content_hash: string, content_type: string}]`
- Content hashes: SHA-256, hex-encoded, lowercase
- Exit codes: 0 (found >=1), 1 (none found), 2 (error)

---

### Story 1.3: Doc tracking at compile time

As a skill maintainer,
I want CS to record detected documentation pages and README with content hashes at compile time,
So that future audits can detect when upstream docs have changed since the skill was compiled.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** a skill brief with a repo URL and doc detection results available from the pipeline
**When** CS compile runs and reaches the doc-sources step
**Then** `metadata.json` contains a `doc_sources` array with entries `{url, detected_via, content_hash, recorded_at}`
**And** `content_hash` values are SHA-256 hex lowercase
**And** `recorded_at` is an ISO-8601 timestamp with timezone

**Given** a compile where doc detection found zero external docs
**When** CS compile runs
**Then** `metadata.json` `doc_sources` still contains at least one entry for the repo's `README.md`
**And** the README entry has `detected_via: "readme_always"`

**Given** a compile where doc detection found three external doc pages
**When** CS compile completes
**Then** `doc_sources` contains exactly four entries (three detected + README)

**Given** doc_sources already exist in metadata.json from a prior compile
**When** CS recompiles the same skill
**Then** `doc_sources` is fully replaced with current detection results (not appended to stale data)

**Technical Notes:**
- New step file: `src/skf-create-skill/references/step-doc-sources.md`
- Step invokes `skf-detect-docs.py` if doc detection was not already run upstream in the pipeline
- Writes to the same `metadata.json` that CS already produces
- README.md is always tracked as a baseline regardless of detection results

---

### Story 1.4: Doc drift detection in audit

As a skill maintainer,
I want AS to compare current documentation hashes against the hashes stored at compile time,
So that I know which upstream docs have changed and can decide whether the skill needs updating.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** a skill with `doc_sources` recorded in `metadata.json` and one tracked doc has changed since compile
**When** `@Ferris AS` runs against that skill
**Then** the audit report includes a doc drift section listing the changed doc with `{url, old_hash, new_hash, detected_at}`
**And** the drift is reported alongside (not instead of) source code drift

**Given** a skill with `doc_sources` and no tracked docs have changed
**When** AS runs
**Then** the doc drift section reports "No documentation drift detected"
**And** no false positives appear

**Given** a skill compiled before Story 1.3 (no `doc_sources` in metadata.json)
**When** AS runs
**Then** the doc drift step is skipped gracefully with a note: "No doc_sources recorded — skip doc drift check"
**And** the rest of the audit proceeds normally

**Given** a tracked doc URL that returns a network error during audit
**When** AS attempts to fetch and hash the doc
**Then** the drift report marks that URL as `"status": "fetch_failed"` instead of reporting false drift
**And** the audit does not abort

**Technical Notes:**
- New step file: `src/skf-audit-skill/references/step-doc-drift.md`
- Step re-fetches each tracked URL, computes SHA-256, compares against stored `content_hash`
- Drift report format: per-doc `{url, old_hash, new_hash, detected_at}` — human-readable markdown output
- No content stored — only hashes compared

---

### Story 1.5: SS active_version manifest-state bugfix

As a stack skill user,
I want SS manifest operations to keep `active_version` consistent after every operation,
So that stranded active_version state does not cause downstream workflows to reference the wrong skill version.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** a manifest with multiple versions where `active_version` points to version A
**When** SS performs a set operation that activates version B
**Then** `active_version` is updated to version B
**And** version A's status is archived
**And** no version other than B has active status

**Given** a manifest where `active_version` was pre-advanced (advanced before the set operation ran)
**When** SS set runs
**Then** the prior genuinely-active version is archived
**And** `active_version` reflects the correct current version
**And** no stranded active state remains

**Given** the existing test suite at `test/test-skf-manifest-ops.py`
**When** `uv run pytest test/test-skf-manifest-ops.py` is executed after the fix
**Then** all existing tests pass (no regressions)
**And** any new regression test covering the stranded-state scenario passes

**Technical Notes:**
- Fix location: `src/shared/scripts/skf-manifest-ops.py`
- Test location: `test/test-skf-manifest-ops.py`
- This is a bugfix, not a new feature — the stranded state was identified during brainstorming (BH-8)
- Verify the fix does not break compose-mode SS flows that rely on manifest operations

---
---

## Epic 2: deepwiki — Zero-Ceremony Skill Creation

**Goal:** Run `deepwiki <repo-url>` and get a 90%-coverage wiki skill in 3-5 minutes.

**FRs:** FR-1, FR-2, FR-4, FR-5, FR-15, FR-20, FR-21, FR-36, FR-22

---

### Story 2.1: Auto-scope mode for AN

As a deepwiki user,
I want AN to automatically scope a repo using shape detection and export surface analysis,
So that the deepwiki pipeline produces a scope without requiring manual input.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** a repo URL with a standard package manifest (package.json, pyproject.toml, or Cargo.toml)
**When** AN runs with the `[auto]` flag passed via pipeline context
**Then** AN invokes `skf-shape-detect.py` and produces a scope from the shape + export surface
**And** no user input is required
**And** the scope output matches AN's existing scope schema

**Given** a repo where shape detection returns `unknown` (exit code 1)
**When** AN runs in auto mode
**Then** AN falls back to the interactive scope definition step
**And** the user is informed: "Auto-scope could not classify this repo — switching to interactive mode"

**Given** AN is invoked without the `[auto]` flag
**When** the user runs `@Ferris AN`
**Then** AN follows its existing interactive flow unchanged
**And** the auto-scope step is not loaded

**Given** AN auto-scope produces a scope for a repo with 200 exports
**When** the scope is passed downstream to BS
**Then** the scope includes export count, shape, and detected signals from shape detection

**Technical Notes:**
- New step files: `src/skf-analyze-source/references/step-auto-scope.md`, `src/skf-analyze-source/references/step-shape-detect.md`
- `[auto]` flag is a pipeline data context modifier, handled by existing pipeline infrastructure
- AN's first step checks for `[auto]` flag and routes to `step-auto-scope.md` or the existing interactive step
- Depends on Story 1.1 (skf-shape-detect.py)

---

### Story 2.2: Auto-brief generation for BS

As a deepwiki user,
I want BS to automatically generate a skill brief from AN auto-scope output and doc detection results,
So that the deepwiki pipeline produces a brief without requiring manual input.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** AN auto-scope output with shape, export count, and scope definition, plus doc detection results
**When** BS runs with the `[auto]` flag passed via pipeline context
**Then** BS generates a structured brief matching the existing BS schema
**And** the brief targets `scope_type: full-library` or `scope_type: public-api` based on the scope
**And** the brief includes detected doc URLs from `skf-detect-docs.py` output

**Given** AN auto-scope output but zero detected documentation sources
**When** BS runs in auto mode
**Then** the brief is still generated (docs are enrichment, not a requirement)
**And** the brief's documentation section is empty or contains only the README reference

**Given** BS is invoked without the `[auto]` flag
**When** the user runs `@Ferris BS`
**Then** BS follows its existing interactive flow unchanged

**Given** a successful auto-brief generation
**When** the brief YAML is written
**Then** it passes validation against the existing brief schema (`skf-validate-brief-schema.py`)

**Technical Notes:**
- New step file: `src/skf-brief-skill/references/step-auto-brief.md`
- BS's first step checks for `[auto]` flag and routes to `step-auto-brief.md` or the existing interactive `gather-intent.md`
- Depends on Story 2.1 (AN auto-scope output) and Story 1.2 (skf-detect-docs.py)
- Auto-brief targets full-library for library-API shapes, public-api for reference-app shapes

---

### Story 2.3: Auto-brief validation with progressive fallback

As a deepwiki user,
I want to review a 10-line summary of the auto-generated brief before CS runs,
So that I can approve, edit, or reject it — and rejection falls back to interactive BS with pre-populated fields.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** BS auto-brief has been generated
**When** the validation step presents the summary to the user
**Then** the summary contains exactly: shape, scope type, export count, doc sources, quality target (10 lines)
**And** the user is offered three actions: `[A]pprove`, `[E]dit`, `[R]eject`

**Given** the user selects `[A]pprove`
**When** validation completes
**Then** the pipeline continues to CS with the auto-generated brief
**And** no further brief interaction occurs

**Given** the user selects `[E]dit`
**When** validation handles the edit
**Then** the user can modify specific fields of the brief
**And** the modified brief is re-validated against the brief schema
**And** the pipeline continues to CS with the edited brief

**Given** the user selects `[R]eject`
**When** validation handles the rejection
**Then** the pipeline does not proceed to CS
**And** interactive BS loads with fields pre-populated from the partial auto-detection results
**And** the user is informed: "Falling back to interactive brief — fields pre-populated from auto-detection"

**Given** the pipeline is running in headless mode
**When** the validation step is reached
**Then** `[A]pprove` is auto-selected (headless default)
**And** the pipeline continues without pausing

**Technical Notes:**
- New step file: `src/skf-brief-skill/references/step-auto-validate.md`
- This step runs between auto-brief generation and CS invocation
- Pre-population on reject: shape, scope type, doc URLs, and export list carry into the interactive flow
- Headless gate type: auto-proceed with `[A]` per headless-gate-convention.md

---

### Story 2.4: Auto-decomposition for massive repos

As a deepwiki user targeting a large repo,
I want AN to automatically recommend multi-skill decomposition when the repo exceeds complexity thresholds,
So that massive repos produce multiple focused skills instead of one unwieldy skill.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** a repo where AN auto-scope detects more than 500 exports
**When** auto-scope runs
**Then** AN recommends a multi-skill decomposition
**And** the recommendation includes suggested skill boundaries

**Given** a repo where AN auto-scope detects more than 3 packages (monorepo/workspace)
**When** auto-scope runs
**Then** AN recommends decomposition with one skill per package (or logical grouping)

**Given** a repo with 200 exports and 1 package
**When** auto-scope runs
**Then** no decomposition is recommended
**And** a single scope is produced

**Given** AN recommends decomposition in the deepwiki pipeline
**When** the pipeline proceeds
**Then** BS creates N briefs (one per recommended skill)
**And** CS compiles N skills
**And** SS auto-composes a stack from the N skills
**And** the user never manually chooses decomposition boundaries

**Given** the decomposition thresholds
**When** reviewing the implementation
**Then** the 500-export and 3-package thresholds are marked `[PENDING VALIDATION]` in step-auto-scope.md

**Technical Notes:**
- Enhancement within `src/skf-analyze-source/references/step-auto-scope.md` (same file as Story 2.1)
- Thresholds are constants marked `[PENDING VALIDATION]` — expected to be tuned after empirical testing
- Multi-brief flow leverages existing CS `--batch` and SS compose-mode
- GAP-2 from architecture: the multi-brief to multi-CS to SS compose chain is step-file-level orchestration within the deepwiki pipeline

---

### Story 2.5: Docs-only deepwiki path

As a developer who wants a skill for a documentation site (no repo),
I want to run `deepwiki <doc-url>` with just a documentation URL,
So that I get a wiki skill built entirely from documentation content.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** a documentation URL (e.g., `https://docs.example.com`) without a repo URL
**When** `deepwiki <doc-url>` is invoked
**Then** the doc detection chain runs against the provided URL as the primary source
**And** a wiki skill is created with `source_type: docs-only` in the brief

**Given** a docs-only invocation
**When** BS generates the brief
**Then** `source_type` is set to `docs-only`
**And** the brief does not require a repo URL
**And** the brief references the documentation URL as the primary source

**Given** a docs-only invocation where the URL is unreachable
**When** the pipeline starts
**Then** the pipeline halts with a clear error: "Documentation URL unreachable: <url>"
**And** exit code reflects the error

**Given** a standard repo URL (e.g., `https://github.com/org/repo`)
**When** `deepwiki` is invoked with that URL
**Then** the standard repo-based deepwiki flow runs (not the docs-only path)

**Technical Notes:**
- Leverages the existing `source_type: docs-only` path already supported by BS
- The detection of URL type (repo vs docs) happens at pipeline entry — AN's first step or the pipeline alias parser
- Doc detection chain (`skf-detect-docs.py`) runs against the URL; the URL itself becomes the primary doc source
- Depends on Story 1.2 (skf-detect-docs.py)

---

### Story 2.6: Coexistence detection

As a deepwiki user,
I want deepwiki to detect existing skills for the same repo/library before generating a new one,
So that I can choose to create alongside, merge, or skip — and never silently overwrite an existing skill.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** existing skills for library `foo` already present in the forge output folder
**When** `deepwiki` targets the same library `foo`
**Then** the user is informed: "Existing skill(s) found for foo: [list]"
**And** the user is offered three actions: `[A]longside` (create wiki variant), `[M]erge` (update existing), `[S]kip`

**Given** the user selects `[A]longside`
**When** the pipeline proceeds
**Then** a new wiki skill is created with a `-wiki` suffix
**And** the existing skill is not modified

**Given** the user selects `[M]erge`
**When** the pipeline proceeds
**Then** the existing skill is updated through the US (Update Skill) workflow
**And** the wiki detection data enriches the existing skill

**Given** the user selects `[S]kip`
**When** the pipeline handles the skip
**Then** no skill is created or modified for that library
**And** the pipeline reports "Skipped: existing skill for foo"

**Given** no existing skills for the targeted library
**When** deepwiki runs
**Then** coexistence detection completes silently
**And** the pipeline proceeds directly to skill creation

**Given** the pipeline is running in headless mode
**When** coexistence is detected
**Then** `[A]longside` is auto-selected (headless default — never silently overwrite)

**Technical Notes:**
- Detection uses `skf-skill-inventory.py` to find existing skills matching the repo URL or package name
- Coexistence check runs early in the deepwiki pipeline, before AN auto-scope
- Headless default is `[A]longside` (safest — creates new, preserves existing)

---

### Story 2.7: Version pinning for deepwiki

As a developer targeting a specific library version,
I want to run `deepwiki <repo-url> --pin <version>` to generate a skill for that exact version,
So that my wiki skill reflects a known release rather than latest HEAD.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** `deepwiki <repo-url> --pin v2.0.0` where `v2.0.0` is a valid semver tag on the repo
**When** the pipeline runs
**Then** AN scopes against the pinned version's code
**And** CS records the pinned version in metadata.json provenance
**And** the generated skill reflects the v2.0.0 API surface

**Given** `deepwiki <repo-url> --pin v99.0.0` where `v99.0.0` does not exist
**When** the pipeline starts
**Then** the pipeline halts with a clear error listing the invalid pin
**And** the error includes suggested corrections (e.g., available tags matching `v99.*`)

**Given** `deepwiki <repo-url>` without `--pin`
**When** the pipeline runs
**Then** the latest release tag is used as the default pin
**And** metadata.json records the resolved version

**Given** `deepwiki <repo-url> --pin main` with a branch name instead of a tag
**When** the pipeline runs
**Then** the branch name is accepted as a valid pin
**And** AN scopes against the branch HEAD

**Given** pin validation logic
**When** comparing with campaign pin validation (FR-32)
**Then** both use the same shared validation module (no duplicate implementation)

**Technical Notes:**
- Pin validation reuses the shared `campaign-validate-pins.py` logic (or a shared extraction thereof)
- `--pin` is consumed by AN's first step, passed downstream to CS for provenance recording
- Default (no --pin): resolve latest release tag via `gh release list --limit 1` or equivalent
- Accepts semver tags, git tags, and branch names

---

### Story 2.8: deepwiki pipeline alias + onboard deprecation

As a user,
I want to type `deepwiki <repo-url>` and have the full wiki-skill pipeline run automatically,
So that I get a high-quality wiki skill with zero ceremony.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the forger SKILL.md pipeline alias table
**When** reviewing the registered aliases
**Then** `deepwiki` is listed and expands to `AN[auto] BS[auto] CS TS[min:90] EX`

**Given** `deepwiki <repo-url>` invoked by the user
**When** the pipeline runs
**Then** AN runs in auto mode, BS runs in auto mode, CS compiles, TS tests at 90% threshold, EX exports
**And** each workflow receives the `[auto]` flag through pipeline data context

**Given** `[auto]` mode flag in a pipeline sequence
**When** the pipeline infrastructure parses `AN[auto]`
**Then** the `auto` flag is set in the workflow data context for AN
**And** AN's first step reads the flag and routes to auto-scope

**Given** `onboard <repo-url>` invoked by the user
**When** the pipeline starts
**Then** a deprecation notice is displayed: "onboard is deprecated — use deepwiki instead"
**And** migration guidance is shown on every invocation
**And** the onboard pipeline runs at 80% threshold (existing behavior)
**And** the pipeline completes despite the deprecation notice

**Given** the pipeline alias table in `src/skf-forger/SKILL.md`
**When** reviewing the Pipeline Mode section
**Then** `deepwiki` appears in the alias expansion list alongside `forge`, `forge-quick`, `onboard`, `maintain`

**Technical Notes:**
- Registration: add `deepwiki` to the alias expansion table in `src/skf-forger/SKILL.md` Pipeline Mode section
- `[auto]` is a new modifier type alongside existing `[min:N]` — pipeline infrastructure already handles bracket modifiers
- Onboard deprecation: modify the `onboard` alias handling to prepend the deprecation notice, keep existing behavior at 80%
- No new pipeline engine — deepwiki uses existing left-to-right chaining, headless auto-proceed, and circuit breaker mechanics

---

### Story 2.9: v1.9 documentation

As a user or contributor,
I want documentation that explains the deepwiki workflow and the onboard deprecation,
So that I can discover and use deepwiki without reading source code.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** `docs/workflows.md`
**When** reviewing the Pipeline Aliases table
**Then** `deepwiki` is listed with its expansion `AN[auto] BS[auto] CS TS[min:90] EX`
**And** `onboard` is marked `(deprecated — use deepwiki)`

**Given** `docs/deepwiki.md`
**When** the file is reviewed
**Then** it contains: purpose, invocation syntax, supported input types (repo URL, doc URL, --pin), pipeline stages, expected output, and timing guidance (3-5 minutes)
**And** it links to workflows.md for pipeline mechanics and concepts.md for terminology

**Given** `docs/bmad-synergy.md`
**When** the file is reviewed
**Then** it includes a "SKF Without BMM" section explaining that deepwiki is the fast-path for users not using the full BMAD Method
**And** deepwiki is positioned as the recommended entry point for standalone SKF usage

**Given** `docs/workflows.md` Feature Workflows section
**When** reviewing workflow listings
**Then** the `onboard` alias entry includes the deprecation notice directing users to `deepwiki`

**Technical Notes:**
- `docs/workflows.md`: update Pipeline Aliases table, add deepwiki to Feature Workflows, mark onboard deprecated
- `docs/deepwiki.md`: new dedicated guide — concise, focused on invocation and expected results
- `docs/bmad-synergy.md`: add "SKF Without BMM" section with deepwiki as the recommended fast-path
- Follow existing docs style: frontmatter with title/description, link to related docs, no inline version stamps

# Epic 3 & 4 — Story Drafts

## Epic 3: Quality, Scale & Workaround Automation

Goal: Higher quality skills with transparent quality gates, automatic handling of large outputs, and no more re-discovering workarounds.

FRs: FR-11, FR-12, FR-13, FR-14, FR-16, FR-17, FR-18, FR-19

---

### Story 3.1: Tiered quality gate — hard gate

As a skill author,
I want the test pipeline to block on unresolved critical/high findings before scoring,
So that no skill with serious defects can pass regardless of its coverage percentage.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** a skill with one or more unresolved critical-severity findings
**When** TS runs the hard gate check (step-hard-gate.md) before scoring
**Then** the pipeline blocks with a clear message listing each critical/high finding
**And** no coverage score is computed or reported

**Given** a skill with one or more unresolved high-severity findings
**When** TS runs the hard gate check
**Then** the pipeline blocks with the same behavior as critical findings

**Given** a skill with only medium and low severity findings
**When** TS runs the hard gate check
**Then** the hard gate passes and the pipeline proceeds to the soft gate (coverage scoring)
**And** medium/low findings are documented in the gap report but do not block

**Deliverables:**
- `src/skf-test-skill/references/step-hard-gate.md`

---

### Story 3.2: Per-pipeline quality thresholds

As a pipeline operator,
I want each pipeline alias to declare its own default quality threshold,
So that deepwiki skills are held to 90% while forge skills default to 80%.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** a TS invocation within the deepwiki pipeline and no explicit override
**When** TS looks up the threshold
**Then** the default threshold is 90%

**Given** a TS invocation within the forge pipeline and no explicit override
**When** TS looks up the threshold
**Then** the default threshold is 80%

**Given** a TS invocation with the `TS[min:85]` override syntax
**When** TS resolves the threshold
**Then** the override value of 85% is used instead of the pipeline default
**And** the source of the threshold (override vs default) is logged

**Given** any pipeline and any threshold configuration
**When** TS runs
**Then** the hard gate (Story 3.1) is enforced before the soft gate regardless of threshold value

**Deliverables:**
- Modified TS first step to include threshold lookup table: `{deepwiki: 90, forge: 80, forge-quick: 80, campaign: 90}`

---

### Story 3.3: Evidence report on threshold fallback

As a skill author,
I want a written evidence report when the soft gate falls back from its target threshold to the 80% floor,
So that the quality compromise is documented and never silent.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** a skill that scores 82% against a 90% target and remediation has been attempted
**When** TS falls back from the 90% target to the 80% floor
**Then** an evidence report is written to `forge-data/{skill-name}/{version}/evidence-report-fallback.md`
**And** the report contains: attempted threshold, achieved score, specific findings preventing the higher threshold, remediation steps attempted, and the final accepted threshold

**Given** a fallback occurs
**When** the pipeline completes
**Then** the fallback is visible in the pipeline output (not buried in the report alone)
**And** the pipeline output includes the path to the evidence report

**Given** a skill that meets its target threshold without fallback
**When** TS completes scoring
**Then** no evidence report is generated

---

### Story 3.4: Auto-shard at 400-line ceiling

As a skill author,
I want SKILL.md bodies exceeding 400 lines to be automatically sharded into references,
So that skills stay within the 500-line skill-check ceiling with comfortable headroom.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** a compiled SKILL.md with a body exceeding 400 lines
**When** the auto-shard step runs post-compile
**Then** navigational catalogs and deep-dive sections are moved to `references/`
**And** the SKILL.md body is reduced to under 400 lines
**And** hallucination guards (T1 markers, confidence annotations) remain inline in SKILL.md

**Given** a compiled SKILL.md with a body of 350 lines
**When** the auto-shard step runs post-compile
**Then** no sharding is triggered and SKILL.md is unchanged

**Given** a sharded SKILL.md
**When** the resulting skill is validated by skill-check
**Then** the skill passes the 500-line ceiling check
**And** cross-references between SKILL.md and sharded reference files are intact

**Deliverables:**
- `src/skf-create-skill/references/step-auto-shard.md`

---

### Story 3.5: Pre-apply workaround registry

As a pipeline operator,
I want known workarounds to be automatically applied before each pipeline iteration,
So that previously-solved issues are not re-discovered and manually fixed each time.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** a `_known-workarounds.yaml` registry with a fingerprint matching content in the target directory
**When** `skf-preapply.py --target-dir <path>` runs
**Then** the matching fix is applied to the target content
**And** the applied fix is logged to `forge-data/{skill-name}/{version}/preapply-log.json`

**Given** a registry with no fingerprints matching the target content
**When** `skf-preapply.py` runs
**Then** no fixes are applied and the log records zero matches

**Given** both a shared seed registry and a project-local registry with a colliding fingerprint
**When** `skf-preapply.py --target-dir <path> --local-registry <path>` runs
**Then** the project-local fix wins on collision
**And** the shared seed is loaded first, project-local merged on top

**Given** a new workaround discovered during a pipeline run
**When** it is recorded
**Then** it is appended to the project-local registry only, never to the shared seed

**Given** a fingerprint entry with `regex: true`
**When** matching is performed
**Then** regex matching is used instead of the default substring matching

**Deliverables:**
- `src/shared/scripts/skf-preapply.py`
- `src/shared/_known-workarounds.yaml` (schema: version 1, workarounds array with fingerprint, fix, source, severity, description)
- Shared seed with 25 GMC campaign fingerprints
- `test/test-skf-preapply.py`

---

### Story 3.6: Doc-rot correction hooks

As a skill author,
I want corrected items in feeder artifacts to be automatically annotated post-compile,
So that doc-rot corrections are surfaced deterministically without relying on AI judgment.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** a feeder artifact containing corrected content (identifiable via grep patterns)
**When** the doc-rot hook fires post-compile
**Then** correction blocks (`## CORRECTION`) are annotated in the compiled output

**Given** the doc-rot hook
**When** it performs matching
**Then** matching is grep-based and deterministic (no AI judgment involved)

**Given** a standalone deepwiki pipeline run (not a campaign)
**When** CS completes compilation
**Then** the doc-rot hook fires (it is not campaign-only)

**Given** a feeder artifact with no corrected content
**When** the doc-rot hook fires
**Then** no correction blocks are added and the output is unchanged

**Deliverables:**
- `src/skf-create-skill/references/step-doc-rot.md`

---

## Epic 4: Campaign Orchestration (v2.0)

Goal: Orchestrate 15+ coordinated skills across multiple sessions with dependency tracking, file-based state, and resume.

FRs: FR-24 through FR-35, FR-23

---

### Story 4.1: Campaign workflow scaffolding + state schema

As a campaign operator,
I want the skf-campaign workflow directory and state schema to exist,
So that campaign orchestration has a well-defined entry point and validated state format.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** the SKF source tree
**When** the campaign workflow is scaffolded
**Then** `src/skf-campaign/` exists with: SKILL.md, manifest.yaml, references/, assets/, templates/, scripts/

**Given** the campaign state schema at `assets/campaign-state-schema.json`
**When** a `_campaign-state.yaml` file is validated against it
**Then** the schema enforces: campaign (name, started_at, last_updated, current_stage, directive_path, quality_gate, health_findings_queue), skills (name, status, depends_on, tier, pin, brief_path, skill_path, quality_score, workarounds_applied, started_at, completed_at), dependency_graph (execution_order, circular_deps_detected)

**Given** any operation that modifies `_campaign-state.yaml`
**When** the modification is about to be written
**Then** a `.bak` copy is written first (read-backup-modify-write pattern)

**Given** the test suite at `test/test-skf-campaign-state.py`
**When** tests run via `uv run`
**Then** all state schema validation and backup behavior tests pass

**Deliverables:**
- `src/skf-campaign/` directory with full structure
- `src/skf-campaign/SKILL.md` with trigger menu (`campaign`), mode routing, resume detection
- `src/skf-campaign/assets/campaign-state-schema.json`
- `test/test-skf-campaign-state.py`

---

### Story 4.2: Campaign setup + campaign-brief.yaml (Stage 0)

As a campaign operator,
I want Stage 0 to create a campaign state file and brief from my inputs,
So that the campaign has a persistent starting point that survives context death.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** target libraries, tiers, and pins provided by the user
**When** the setup step runs
**Then** `_campaign-state.yaml` is created and validates against the schema
**And** `campaign-brief.yaml` is generated from `templates/campaign-brief-template.yaml`

**Given** no explicit health_findings_queue configuration
**When** setup runs
**Then** health_findings_queue defaults to local (project-local queue)
**And** a one-time opt-in prompt is offered for the shared queue

**Given** the generated state file
**When** validated against `assets/campaign-state-schema.json`
**Then** validation passes with no errors

**Deliverables:**
- `src/skf-campaign/references/step-01-setup.md`
- `src/skf-campaign/templates/campaign-brief-template.yaml`

---

### Story 4.3: Strategy generation + provenance (Stages 1 + 3)

As a campaign operator,
I want a human-readable strategy view and verified source provenance,
So that I can review the plan before execution and trust that all targets are accessible.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** a state file with targets populated from Stage 0
**When** the strategy step runs
**Then** a human-readable strategy view is generated showing skills, tiers, dependencies, and execution order

**Given** a state file with targets
**When** the provenance step runs
**Then** all target repos are verified accessible via gh
**And** commit SHAs are recorded in the state file for each target
**And** inaccessible repos produce a clear error and block the campaign

**Deliverables:**
- `src/skf-campaign/references/step-02-strategy.md`
- `src/skf-campaign/references/step-04-provenance.md`

---

### Story 4.4: Pin validation (Stage 2)

As a campaign operator,
I want version pins validated against actual releases before the campaign begins,
So that invalid pins are caught early with actionable suggestions.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** skills with valid version pins in the state file
**When** `campaign-validate-pins.py` runs
**Then** all pins are confirmed against gh releases and package registries
**And** the validation result is recorded in the state file

**Given** a skill with an invalid version pin
**When** validation runs
**Then** a clear error is reported with suggested corrections (nearest valid versions)

**Given** a deepwiki invocation with `--pin <version>`
**When** pin validation runs
**Then** the same `campaign-validate-pins.py` validator is used (shared infrastructure with FR-36)

**Given** the test suite at `test/test-skf-campaign-pins.py`
**When** tests run via `uv run`
**Then** all pin validation tests pass

**Deliverables:**
- `src/skf-campaign/references/step-03-pins.md`
- `src/skf-campaign/scripts/campaign-validate-pins.py`
- `test/test-skf-campaign-pins.py`

---

### Story 4.5: Dependency tracking + enforcement

As a campaign operator,
I want skills with declared dependencies to execute in the correct order,
So that no skill starts before its prerequisites have passed.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** skills with `depends_on` edges declared in the state file
**When** the campaign computes execution order
**Then** a topological sort determines the correct sequence
**And** the execution_order is written to the state file's dependency_graph

**Given** a circular dependency in the declared edges
**When** the campaign attempts to compute execution order at setup time
**Then** the circular dependency is detected and rejected with a clear error listing the cycle

**Given** a skill whose dependency has not yet completed (status != completed)
**When** the campaign attempts to start that skill
**Then** the skill is blocked with a clear message naming the unmet dependency
**And** a manual override flag is available to force execution

---

### Story 4.6: Per-skill loop + kickoff emitter (Stage 4)

As a campaign operator,
I want each skill processed in dependency order with a fresh-context kickoff message,
So that every skill iteration starts with the right context regardless of session boundaries.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** 3 skills in computed execution order
**When** the skill loop runs
**Then** each skill is processed in order: pre-apply, kickoff emit, AN, BS, CS, TS, mark complete, propagate findings, doc-rot check

**Given** a skill entering the loop
**When** the kickoff emitter runs
**Then** a kickoff message is generated from `templates/kickoff-template.md` containing: skill name, brief summary, dependency state, directive, workarounds applied

**Given** a skill completing successfully
**When** its state is updated
**Then** the status transitions atomically from `active` to `completed`
**And** `completed_at` timestamp and `quality_score` are recorded

**Given** a skill that fails during processing
**When** its state is updated
**Then** the status transitions atomically from `active` to `failed`
**And** downstream skills that do not depend on it continue processing

**Deliverables:**
- `src/skf-campaign/references/step-05-skill-loop.md`
- `src/skf-campaign/templates/kickoff-template.md`

---

### Story 4.7: Tier B batch + capstone (Stages 5-6)

As a campaign operator,
I want Tier B skills batched through QS and a capstone stack skill composed from completed skills,
So that lower-tier skills are handled efficiently and the final stack integrates everything.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** Tier B skills in the campaign state
**When** the batch stage runs
**Then** QS `--batch` is invoked for all Tier B skills

**Given** completed individual skills from the skill loop and batch stages
**When** the capstone stage runs
**Then** SS compose-mode is invoked to produce a stack skill from the completed skills
**And** the stack skill quality is recorded in the campaign state

**Deliverables:**
- `src/skf-campaign/references/step-06-batch.md`
- `src/skf-campaign/references/step-07-capstone.md`

---

### Story 4.8: Verification + refinement (Stages 7-8)

As a campaign operator,
I want generated skills verified and refined before export,
So that the final skill set meets quality standards with architecture-aware improvements.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** completed skills from the loop, batch, and capstone stages
**When** the verification stage runs
**Then** VS produces a verification report covering all generated skills

**Given** a VS verification report
**When** the refinement stage runs
**Then** RA improvements are applied to the architecture document and relevant skills
**And** refinement results are recorded in the campaign state

**Deliverables:**
- `src/skf-campaign/references/step-08-verify.md`
- `src/skf-campaign/references/step-09-refine.md`

---

### Story 4.9: Export + maintenance + campaign report (Stages 9-10)

As a campaign operator,
I want a gated export with a comprehensive campaign report,
So that no skills are exported without confirmation and the full campaign is auditable.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** all prior stages completed
**When** the export stage runs
**Then** a single write-gate HALT is presented requiring explicit user confirmation before any files are written

**Given** all stages completed and export confirmed
**When** the maintenance stage runs
**Then** a campaign report is generated from `templates/campaign-report-template.md` containing: skills completed, quality scores per skill, findings summary, workarounds applied, doc-rot corrections, total duration
**And** the shared health check is chained after report generation

**Given** the campaign report
**When** it is written
**Then** it is placed at a predictable path in `forge-data/` and referenced from the campaign state

**Deliverables:**
- `src/skf-campaign/references/step-10-export.md`
- `src/skf-campaign/references/step-11-maintenance.md`
- `src/skf-campaign/scripts/campaign-report.py`
- `src/skf-campaign/templates/campaign-report-template.md`

---

### Story 4.10: Campaign resume

As a campaign operator,
I want to resume a campaign from any skill after a session boundary,
So that context death, multi-day runs, and interruptions do not lose progress.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** a campaign with 5 of 10 skills completed in `_campaign-state.yaml`
**When** `campaign resume --from=skill-6` is invoked
**Then** skills 1 through 5 are skipped (their completed status is preserved)
**And** the named skill becomes the entry point for the skill loop

**Given** a resume invocation
**When** the state file is loaded
**Then** state integrity is validated against the schema before resuming
**And** the `.bak` file is checked for consistency

**Given** a campaign that was interrupted mid-skill (status = active)
**When** resume is invoked without `--from`
**Then** the campaign resumes from the last active skill

**Given** a campaign state file written days ago in a different session
**When** resume is invoked
**Then** the campaign resumes correctly (no in-memory state dependency per NFR-2)

---

### Story 4.11: Standing directive

As a campaign operator,
I want a file-based directive that the campaign reads at every stage transition,
So that I can adjust campaign-wide policy without editing the state file or relying on LLM memory.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** a `_campaign-directive.md` file in the campaign working directory
**When** the campaign transitions between stages
**Then** the directive file is read and its contents applied to the current stage

**Given** no `_campaign-directive.md` file present
**When** the campaign transitions between stages
**Then** the campaign proceeds with default behavior and no error

**Given** a directive file
**When** it is inspected
**Then** it is a plain markdown file that is auditable and version-controllable
**And** it is file-based with no memory dependency (per NFR-2)

**Deliverables:**
- `src/skf-campaign/references/campaign-directive-spec.md`

---

### Story 4.12: onboard removal + v2.0 documentation

As a user of SKF v2.0,
I want the deprecated onboard alias removed and campaign documentation in place,
So that the migration to deepwiki is complete and the 15th workflow is documented.

**Acceptance Criteria:**

**Given** a user invoking `onboard` with SKF v2.0
**When** Ferris processes the command
**Then** a clear error message is returned with a pointer to `deepwiki` as the replacement

**Given** the forger SKILL.md
**When** v2.0 is released
**Then** the `onboard` alias is removed from the trigger menu

1	# Story 1.1: Skill-Shape Detection Shared Module
2	
3	Status: done
4	
5	<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->
6	
7	## Story
8	
9	As a pipeline author,
10	I want a standalone shape-detection script that classifies repos into known skill shapes,
11	so that AN auto-scope, BS auto-brief, and TS threshold selection can all consume the same classification without duplicating heuristics.
12	
13	## Acceptance Criteria
14	
15	1. **Given** a repo URL and one or more manifest paths (package.json, pyproject.toml, Cargo.toml), **When** `uv run python src/shared/scripts/skf-shape-detect.py --repo-url <url> --manifests <paths>` is invoked, **Then** stdout contains a JSON object `{shape, signals[], confidence, export_count, package_count}`, `shape` is one of `library-API`, `reference-app`, `language-reference`, `stack-compose`, and exit code is `0`.
16	
17	2. **Given** a repo whose manifests and structure do not match any known shape, **When** shape detection is invoked, **Then** `shape` is `unknown`, and exit code is `1`.
18	
19	3. **Given** an invalid repo URL or missing manifest paths, **When** shape detection is invoked, **Then** stderr contains `{"error": "...", "code": "ERROR_TYPE"}` and exit code is `2`.
20	
21	4. **Given** the test suite at `test/test-skf-shape-detect.py`, **When** `uv run pytest test/test-skf-shape-detect.py` is executed, **Then** all tests pass and the test file is registered in `package.json` `test:python` script.
22	
23	## Tasks / Subtasks
24	
25	- [x] Task 1: Create `src/shared/scripts/skf-shape-detect.py` (AC: #1, #2, #3)
26	  - [x] 1.1 PEP 723 header (`requires-python = ">=3.9"`, `dependencies = []`)
27	  - [x] 1.2 Module-level docstring: purpose, CLI syntax, input args, output schema, exit codes
28	  - [x] 1.3 Argparse: `--repo-url <url>` (required), `--manifests <path1,path2,...>` (required, comma-separated)
29	  - [x] 1.4 Implement `detect(repo_url, manifest_paths)` as the pure-logic entry point (testable without subprocess)
30	  - [x] 1.5 Manifest file reading and parsing (JSON for package.json, TOML for pyproject.toml/Cargo.toml)
31	  - [x] 1.6 Classification heuristic ladder (first match wins, see Dev Notes)
32	  - [x] 1.7 Signals array: accumulate evidence strings during classification
33	  - [x] 1.8 Confidence scoring: float 0.0–1.0 based on signal count and strength
34	  - [x] 1.9 JSON stdout output: `{shape, signals, confidence, export_count, package_count}`
35	  - [x] 1.10 Error handling: stderr JSON `{"error": "...", "code": "ERROR_TYPE"}`, exit 2
36	  - [x] 1.11 Unknown fallback: exit 1 when no shape matches
37	- [x] Task 2: Create `test/test-skf-shape-detect.py` (AC: #4)
38	  - [x] 2.1 Import via `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location` (follow skf-detect-language.py pattern)
39	  - [x] 2.2 `assert_result_shape()` helper validating output schema keys and types
40	  - [x] 2.3 Pure-function tests for each shape: library-API, reference-app, language-reference, stack-compose
41	  - [x] 2.4 Unknown shape test: manifests that don't match any heuristic → exit 1
42	  - [x] 2.5 Error tests: missing manifests, invalid inputs → exit 2
43	  - [x] 2.6 Subprocess tests: verify CLI wiring (argparse, stdout JSON, exit codes)
44	  - [x] 2.7 Edge cases: empty manifest, manifest with no exports, monorepo manifests
45	- [x] Task 3: Register test in `package.json` `test:python` script (AC: #4)
46	  - [x] 3.1 Append `test/test-skf-shape-detect.py` to the `test:python` command in `package.json`
47	
48	## Dev Notes
49	
50	### Shape Classification Heuristics
51	
52	The architecture specifies four shapes plus `unknown`. The heuristic ladder classifies based on manifest content and structure signals. Proposed classification rules (the dev agent should refine based on the signals available from parsed manifests):
53	
54	| Shape | Key Signals | Confidence |
55	|-------|------------|------------|
56	| `library-API` | High export count (>50 named exports), no `bin` field in package.json, `main`/`module`/`exports` fields present | 0.85–0.95 |
57	| `reference-app` | Has `bin` field OR framework entry point (Next.js `pages/`, Express `server.ts/js`), low export count | 0.80–0.90 |
58	| `language-reference` | Grammar files (`.g4`, `.pest`, `.lalrpop`), parser-related deps, AST-related exports | 0.75–0.85 |
59	| `stack-compose` | Multiple manifests (>1 ecosystem), Docker/docker-compose presence, monorepo layout | 0.80–0.90 |
60	| `unknown` | No strong signals, conflicting signals, or insufficient data | 0.0–0.3 |
61	
62	Signals array examples: `["has_package_json", "exports_count_gt_50", "no_bin_field", "has_main_field"]`
63	
64	### Relationship to scope_type (DO NOT CONFUSE)
65	
66	`shape` and `scope_type` are **different enums at different classification levels**:
67	- **shape** (this script): Repo-level structural classification — *what kind of project is this?* Values: `library-API`, `reference-app`, `language-reference`, `stack-compose`, `unknown`
68	- **scope_type** (skf-recommend-scope-type.py): Brief-level scoping — *how should we extract knowledge from it?* Values: `full-library`, `specific-modules`, `public-api`, `component-library`, `reference-app`, `docs-only`
69	
70	Note: `reference-app` appears in both enums with consistent meaning. Shape informs downstream scope_type selection but they are independent scripts with independent output schemas.
71	
72	### Manifest Parsing
73	
74	The `--manifests` flag receives comma-separated **local file paths** to already-cloned manifest files. The script does NOT clone repos — the caller (AN step file) provides local paths after cloning.
75	
76	Supported manifests to parse:
77	- `package.json` — parse JSON, extract: `name`, `version`, `main`, `module`, `exports`, `bin`, `dependencies`, `devDependencies`, `peerDependencies`
78	- `pyproject.toml` — parse TOML (use `tomllib` stdlib ≥3.11 or manual parsing for 3.9 compat), extract: `[project]` name/version/dependencies, `[tool.poetry.dependencies]`
79	- `Cargo.toml` — parse TOML, extract: `[package]` name/version, `[dependencies]`, `[workspace]` members
80	
81	For Python 3.9 compatibility with TOML parsing: use a minimal TOML parser or restrict to the subset needed. Do NOT add external dependencies (keep `dependencies = []`).
82	
83	### Export Count Computation
84	
85	`export_count` is the number of public-facing exports from the manifest:
86	- **npm**: Count keys in `exports` field; fallback to counting files in `main`/`module` if no `exports` field
87	- **Python**: Count entry points from `[project.scripts]` + `[project.gui-scripts]`; fallback to package name count
88	- **Rust**: Count `[lib]` + `[[bin]]` targets
89	
90	`package_count` is the number of distinct packages/crates detected across all provided manifests.
91	
92	### Project Structure Notes
93	
94	- **File location**: `src/shared/scripts/skf-shape-detect.py` — shared because AN, BS, and TS all consume it
95	- **Test location**: `test/test-skf-shape-detect.py` — follows 1:1 test↔script convention
96	- **No workflow step files**: This story creates only the shared script; AN/BS/TS step files that invoke it are in stories 2.1, 2.2, and 3.2
97	
98	### Existing Script Patterns to Follow
99	
100	Model after `skf-detect-language.py` and `skf-recommend-scope-type.py`:
101	
102	1. **PEP 723 header**: `# /// script` block with `requires-python` and `dependencies`
103	2. **Module docstring**: Full CLI docs, input/output schemas, exit codes
104	3. **Pure-logic function**: `detect(repo_url, manifest_paths)` → dict (testable without subprocess)
105	4. **argparse CLI**: `--repo-url` and `--manifests` flags (GNU-style long flags, kebab-case)
106	5. **JSON stdout**: Single JSON object, no pretty-print
107	6. **stderr for errors**: `{"error": "...", "code": "ERROR_TYPE"}` on exit 2
108	7. **Exit codes**: 0 (classified), 1 (unknown shape), 2 (error)
109	8. **Encoding**: Use `encoding="utf-8"` on all `open()` calls (cross-platform requirement)
110	9. **Paths**: Use `Path.as_posix()` not `str()` for any paths in JSON output
111	
112	### Test Patterns to Follow
113	
114	Model after `test/test-skf-detect-language.py`:
115	
116	1. **Import via importlib**: `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("skf_shape_detect", SCRIPT_PATH)`
117	2. **SCRIPT_PATH**: `Path(__file__).parent.parent / "src" / "shared" / "scripts" / "skf-shape-detect.py"`
118	3. **assert_result_shape()**: Validate all required keys and types
119	4. **Pure-function tests**: Call `mod.detect(...)` directly with fixture data
120	5. **Subprocess tests**: At least 2-3 tests using `subprocess.run(["uv", "run", "python", str(SCRIPT_PATH), ...])` to verify CLI wiring
121	6. **Fixture strategy**: Use `tmp_path` for creating mock manifest files with known content
122	
123	### Cross-Platform Requirements
124	
125	Per project convention (PRs #311/#316/#366/#368/#387):
126	- Use `Path.as_posix()` for any paths in JSON output (never raw `str()`)
127	- Use `encoding="utf-8"` on all file `open()` calls
128	- If writing files, use `os.open` with `O_BINARY` flag
129	- CI gatekeeper: `windows-latest` — tests must pass cross-platform
130	
131	### Architecture References
132	
133	- Shared module interface: [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#Shared Module Interfaces]
134	- Shape enum and signals: [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#Module & File Structure]
135	- Implementation priority: [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#Decision Impact Analysis] — "Shared modules first (detect-docs, shape-detect, pre-apply) — no dependencies"
136	- Script conventions: [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#Shared Script Patterns]
137	
138	### PRD References
139	
140	- FR-3 Shape Detection: [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/prds/prd-bmad-module-skill-forge-2026-05-25/prd.md#§4.1]
141	- Four shapes: `library-API`, `reference-app/CLI`, `language-reference`, `stack/compose`
142	- Assumption: Shape detection succeeds for 90%+ of repos with standard package manifests
143	
144	### Cross-Story Dependencies (Downstream Consumers)
145	
146	This script is a foundational dependency consumed by:
147	- **Story 2.1** (Auto-scope for AN): Invokes shape-detect to classify repos
148	- **Story 2.2** (Auto-brief for BS): Uses shape classification for brief generation strategy
149	- **Story 3.2** (Per-pipeline quality thresholds): TS uses shape for threshold selection
150	- **Epic 4** (Campaign): Shape classification per-skill in campaign loop
151	
152	The output schema `{shape, signals, confidence, export_count, package_count}` is a contract — changing it later requires updating all consumers.
153	
154	### Git Workflow
155	
156	- **Branch from**: `main` at current HEAD
157	- **Branch name**: `source-intelligence-foundation` (epic-level branch; all Epic 1 stories commit here)
158	- **PR target**: `main`
159	- **Epic-level PR**: Single PR opened when Epic 1 is complete
160	
161	### References
162	
163	- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#Shared Module Interfaces] — skf-shape-detect.py interface spec
164	- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/architecture.md#Shared Script Patterns] — naming, args, output, exit codes
165	- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/epics.md#Epic 1 Story 1.1] — acceptance criteria and technical notes
166	- [Source: _bmad-output/planning-artifacts/prds/prd-bmad-module-skill-forge-2026-05-25/prd.md#§4.1 FR-3] — shape definitions
167	- [Source: src/shared/scripts/skf-detect-language.py] — reference implementation for shared script pattern
168	- [Source: test/test-skf-detect-language.py] — reference implementation for test pattern
169	- [Source: src/shared/scripts/skf-recommend-scope-type.py] — heuristic ladder pattern (different enum, same approach)
170	
171	## Dev Agent Record
172	
173	### Agent Model Used
174	
175	Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context)
176	
177	### Debug Log References
178	
179	None — all 40 tests passed on first run, full suite (schemas, install, CLI, workflow, Python, knowledge, validation, lint, format) passed clean.
180	
181	### Completion Notes List
182	
183	- Implemented `skf-shape-detect.py` with 5-shape heuristic ladder: language-reference → stack-compose → reference-app → library-API → unknown
184	- TOML parsing: primary `tomllib` (Python 3.11+) with full fallback parser for Python 3.9 compat (handles tables, arrays, inline tables, multi-line arrays)
185	- Manifest parsers for package.json (JSON), pyproject.toml (TOML), Cargo.toml (TOML) extract ecosystem, deps, export count, library structure, and bin presence
186	- Parser deps (antlr4, pest, tree-sitter, lark, etc.) and framework deps (next, express, fastapi, axum, etc.) as shape signals
187	- Python `[project.scripts]` entries intentionally NOT treated as `has_bin` — they contribute to export_count only, avoiding false reference-app classification for library CLIs (e.g. pytest)
188	- Confidence scoring: shape-specific base + signal-strength bonuses, clamped to documented ranges
189	- 40 tests covering all shapes, errors, edge cases, and CLI wiring (6 subprocess tests)
190	- Registered in `package.json` test:python script
191	
192	### Change Log
193	
194	- 2026-05-26: Story implemented — created skf-shape-detect.py, test-skf-shape-detect.py, registered test in package.json
195	
196	### Review Findings
197	
198	- [x] [Review][Patch] Non-object JSON root in package.json crashes with unhandled AttributeError [skf-shape-detect.py:279]
199	- [x] [Review][Patch] Unused `import re` — dead import [skf-shape-detect.py:51]
200	- [x] [Review][Patch] String-valued `"exports"` field yields export_count=0 despite valid Node.js exports [skf-shape-detect.py:291]
201	- [x] [Review][Patch] Dead conditional `if not has_bin` always True in library-API branch [skf-shape-detect.py:536]
202	- [x] [Review][Defer] Fallback TOML parser does not handle escape sequences in strings [skf-shape-detect.py:176] — deferred, fallback path (Python <3.11 only)
203	- [x] [Review][Defer] `_split_preserving_nesting` does not handle escaped quotes [skf-shape-detect.py:143] — deferred, fallback path (Python <3.11 only)
204	- [x] [Review][Defer] Multi-line array collection breaks on `]` inside string values e.g. PEP 508 extras [skf-shape-detect.py:275] — deferred, fallback path (Python <3.11 only)
205	- [x] [Review][Defer] Cargo `[workspace.dependencies]` are ignored — only top-level dep sections parsed [skf-shape-detect.py:398] — deferred, spec does not require workspace.dependencies extraction
206	
207	### File List
208	
209	- src/shared/scripts/skf-shape-detect.py (new)
210	- test/test-skf-shape-detect.py (new)
211	- package.json (modified — test:python registration)

# Story 1.1: Skill-Shape Detection Shared Module

Status: done

<!-- Note: Validation is optional. Run validate-create-story for quality check before dev-story. -->

## Story

As a pipeline author,
I want a standalone shape-detection script that classifies repos into known skill shapes,
so that AN auto-scope, BS auto-brief, and TS threshold selection can all consume the same classification without duplicating heuristics.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. **Given** a repo URL and one or more manifest paths (package.json, pyproject.toml, Cargo.toml), **When** `uv run python src/shared/scripts/skf-shape-detect.py --repo-url <url> --manifests <paths>` is invoked, **Then** stdout contains a JSON object `{shape, signals[], confidence, export_count, package_count}`, `shape` is one of `library-API`, `reference-app`, `language-reference`, `stack-compose`, and exit code is `0`.

2. **Given** a repo whose manifests and structure do not match any known shape, **When** shape detection is invoked, **Then** `shape` is `unknown`, and exit code is `1`.

3. **Given** an invalid repo URL or missing manifest paths, **When** shape detection is invoked, **Then** stderr contains `{"error": "...", "code": "ERROR_TYPE"}` and exit code is `2`.

4. **Given** the test suite at `test/test-skf-shape-detect.py`, **When** `uv run pytest test/test-skf-shape-detect.py` is executed, **Then** all tests pass and the test file is registered in `package.json` `test:python` script.

## Tasks / Subtasks

- [x] Task 1: Create `src/shared/scripts/skf-shape-detect.py` (AC: #1, #2, #3)
  - [x] 1.1 PEP 723 header (`requires-python = ">=3.9"`, `dependencies = []`)
  - [x] 1.2 Module-level docstring: purpose, CLI syntax, input args, output schema, exit codes
  - [x] 1.3 Argparse: `--repo-url <url>` (required), `--manifests <path1,path2,...>` (required, comma-separated)
  - [x] 1.4 Implement `detect(repo_url, manifest_paths)` as the pure-logic entry point (testable without subprocess)
  - [x] 1.5 Manifest file reading and parsing (JSON for package.json, TOML for pyproject.toml/Cargo.toml)
  - [x] 1.6 Classification heuristic ladder (first match wins, see Dev Notes)
  - [x] 1.7 Signals array: accumulate evidence strings during classification
  - [x] 1.8 Confidence scoring: float 0.0–1.0 based on signal count and strength
  - [x] 1.9 JSON stdout output: `{shape, signals, confidence, export_count, package_count}`
  - [x] 1.10 Error handling: stderr JSON `{"error": "...", "code": "ERROR_TYPE"}`, exit 2
  - [x] 1.11 Unknown fallback: exit 1 when no shape matches
- [x] Task 2: Create `test/test-skf-shape-detect.py` (AC: #4)
  - [x] 2.1 Import via `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location` (follow skf-detect-language.py pattern)
  - [x] 2.2 `assert_result_shape()` helper validating output schema keys and types
  - [x] 2.3 Pure-function tests for each shape: library-API, reference-app, language-reference, stack-compose
  - [x] 2.4 Unknown shape test: manifests that don't match any heuristic → exit 1
  - [x] 2.5 Error tests: missing manifests, invalid inputs → exit 2
  - [x] 2.6 Subprocess tests: verify CLI wiring (argparse, stdout JSON, exit codes)
  - [x] 2.7 Edge cases: empty manifest, manifest with no exports, monorepo manifests
- [x] Task 3: Register test in `package.json` `test:python` script (AC: #4)
  - [x] 3.1 Append `test/test-skf-shape-detect.py` to the `test:python` command in `package.json`

## Dev Notes

### Shape Classification Heuristics

The architecture specifies four shapes plus `unknown`. The heuristic ladder classifies based on manifest content and structure signals. Proposed classification rules (the dev agent should refine based on the signals available from parsed manifests):

| Shape | Key Signals | Confidence |
|-------|------------|------------|
| `library-API` | High export count (>50 named exports), no `bin` field in package.json, `main`/`module`/`exports` fields present | 0.85–0.95 |
| `reference-app` | Has `bin` field OR framework entry point (Next.js `pages/`, Express `server.ts/js`), low export count | 0.80–0.90 |
| `language-reference` | Grammar files (`.g4`, `.pest`, `.lalrpop`), parser-related deps, AST-related exports | 0.75–0.85 |
| `stack-compose` | Multiple manifests (>1 ecosystem), Docker/docker-compose presence, monorepo layout | 0.80–0.90 |
| `unknown` | No strong signals, conflicting signals, or insufficient data | 0.0–0.3 |

Signals array examples: `["has_package_json", "exports_count_gt_50", "no_bin_field", "has_main_field"]`

### Relationship to scope_type (DO NOT CONFUSE)

`shape` and `scope_type` are **different enums at different classification levels**:
- **shape** (this script): Repo-level structural classification — *what kind of project is this?* Values: `library-API`, `reference-app`, `language-reference`, `stack-compose`, `unknown`
- **scope_type** (skf-recommend-scope-type.py): Brief-level scoping — *how should we extract knowledge from it?* Values: `full-library`, `specific-modules`, `public-api`, `component-library`, `reference-app`, `docs-only`

Note: `reference-app` appears in both enums with consistent meaning. Shape informs downstream scope_type selection but they are independent scripts with independent output schemas.

### Manifest Parsing

The `--manifests` flag receives comma-separated **local file paths** to already-cloned manifest files. The script does NOT clone repos — the caller (AN step file) provides local paths after cloning.

Supported manifests to parse:
- `package.json` — parse JSON, extract: `name`, `version`, `main`, `module`, `exports`, `bin`, `dependencies`, `devDependencies`, `peerDependencies`
- `pyproject.toml` — parse TOML (use `tomllib` stdlib ≥3.11 or manual parsing for 3.9 compat), extract: `[project]` name/version/dependencies, `[tool.poetry.dependencies]`
- `Cargo.toml` — parse TOML, extract: `[package]` name/version, `[dependencies]`, `[workspace]` members

For Python 3.9 compatibility with TOML parsing: use a minimal TOML parser or restrict to the subset needed. Do NOT add external dependencies (keep `dependencies = []`).

### Export Count Computation

`export_count` is the number of public-facing exports from the manifest:
- **npm**: Count keys in `exports` field; fallback to counting files in `main`/`module` if no `exports` field
- **Python**: Count entry points from `[project.scripts]` + `[project.gui-scripts]`; fallback to package name count
- **Rust**: Count `[lib]` + `[[bin]]` targets

`package_count` is the number of distinct packages/crates detected across all provided manifests.

### Project Structure Notes

- **File location**: `src/shared/scripts/skf-shape-detect.py` — shared because AN, BS, and TS all consume it
- **Test location**: `test/test-skf-shape-detect.py` — follows 1:1 test↔script convention
- **No workflow step files**: This story creates only the shared script; AN/BS/TS step files that invoke it are in stories 2.1, 2.2, and 3.2

### Existing Script Patterns to Follow

Model after `skf-detect-language.py` and `skf-recommend-scope-type.py`:

1. **PEP 723 header**: `# /// script` block with `requires-python` and `dependencies`
2. **Module docstring**: Full CLI docs, input/output schemas, exit codes
3. **Pure-logic function**: `detect(repo_url, manifest_paths)` → dict (testable without subprocess)
4. **argparse CLI**: `--repo-url` and `--manifests` flags (GNU-style long flags, kebab-case)
5. **JSON stdout**: Single JSON object, no pretty-print
6. **stderr for errors**: `{"error": "...", "code": "ERROR_TYPE"}` on exit 2
7. **Exit codes**: 0 (classified), 1 (unknown shape), 2 (error)
8. **Encoding**: Use `encoding="utf-8"` on all `open()` calls (cross-platform requirement)
9. **Paths**: Use `Path.as_posix()` not `str()` for any paths in JSON output

### Test Patterns to Follow

Model after `test/test-skf-detect-language.py`:

1. **Import via importlib**: `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location("skf_shape_detect", SCRIPT_PATH)`
2. **SCRIPT_PATH**: `Path(__file__).parent.parent / "src" / "shared" / "scripts" / "skf-shape-detect.py"`
3. **assert_result_shape()**: Validate all required keys and types
4. **Pure-function tests**: Call `mod.detect(...)` directly with fixture data
5. **Subprocess tests**: At least 2-3 tests using `subprocess.run(["uv", "run", "python", str(SCRIPT_PATH), ...])` to verify CLI wiring
6. **Fixture strategy**: Use `tmp_path` for creating mock manifest files with known content

### Cross-Platform Requirements

Per project convention (PRs #311/#316/#366/#368/#387):

5	# Story 1.2: Doc Detection Chain Shared Module
6	
7	Status: done

10	
11	## Story
12	
13	As a pipeline author,
14	I want a standalone doc-detection script that discovers documentation URLs through multiple methods,
15	so that BS, CS, AS, and campaign workflows can all locate a repo's docs without duplicating detection logic.
16	
17	## Acceptance Criteria
18	
19	1. **Given** a repo URL with a `homepageUrl` set on GitHub, **When** `uv run python src/shared/scripts/skf-detect-docs.py --repo-url <url>` is invoked, **Then** stdout contains a JSON array including an entry with `detected_via: "homepageUrl"`, and exit code is `0`.
20	
21	2. **Given** a repo whose README contains documentation links, **When** doc detection is invoked, **Then** the output array includes entries with `detected_via: "readme_link"`, and each entry has `url`, `content_hash` (SHA-256 with `sha256:` prefix, hex lowercase), and `content_type`.
22	
23	3. **Given** a repo with a GitHub Pages site, **When** doc detection is invoked without `--skip-pages-api`, **Then** the output array includes an entry with `detected_via: "pages_api"`.
24	
25	4. **Given** a repo with a `docs/` folder containing markdown files, **When** doc detection is invoked, **Then** the output array includes entries with `detected_via: "docs_folder"`.
26	
27	5. **Given** a repo whose README links to a CHANGELOG.md or migration guide, **When** doc detection is invoked, **Then** changelogs and migration guides are excluded from the output array.
28	
29	6. **Given** all four detection methods find zero documentation sources, **When** doc detection is invoked, **Then** stdout contains an empty JSON array `[]`, and exit code is `1`.
30	
31	7. **Given** a `--local-path` argument alongside `--repo-url`, **When** doc detection is invoked, **Then** docs/ folder detection uses the local path instead of cloning.
32	
33	8. **Given** the test suite at `test/test-skf-detect-docs.py`, **When** `uv run pytest test/test-skf-detect-docs.py` is executed, **Then** all tests pass and the test file is registered in `package.json` `test:python` script.
34
